subject: Biology
title: Mitosis: Copying the Blueprint
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Mitosis: Copying the Blueprint.

hook.text: When a cell divides by mitosis, it creates two identical copies of itself.

buildup.text: First, every chromosome is duplicated. Then the cell pulls copies to opposite sides.

discovery.text: Four phases drive the process: prophase, metaphase, anaphase, and telophase.

twist.text: Plant cells can't pinch apart like animal cells. They build a new wall down the middle.

climax.text: Mitosis replaces your skin, blood, and tissue with perfect genetic copies every day.

punchline.text: Two cells. Same DNA. Perfect copies.
